title: impatiens brevicornis
intro: Impatiens brevicornis, also known as the dwarf jewelweed, is a plant species in the balsam family (Balsamaceae) that is native to tropical Africa. The leaves and stems of the plant contain a compound called lawsone, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. Impatiens brevicornis has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and fever, due to its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
